title: Building a Minimalist Triple-Monitor Setup Without the Cable Mess
summary: Running dual-laptop, multi-monitor workflows without visual noise comes down to two things: real Thunderbolt bandwidth and a deliberate cable plan from the start.
content: A minimalist desk setup isn't really about owning fewer things — it's about making the things you do own invisible until you need them. The biggest threat to that is bandwidth-starved docking stations that force you to choose between display count and data throughput.

gear_list.name: Logitech Lift Vertical Ergonomic Wireless Mouse

title: The Everyday Carry Setup for Remote Work That Travels
summary: Moving between coffee shops, client sites, and home doesn't have to mean carrying a different version of your setup each time — here's what actually earns a permanent spot in the bag.
content: Everyday carry tech fails when it's chosen for how it looks rather than how often it's actually used. The real test is whether something survives three months of daily transitions between locations without you replacing it out of frustration.

title: The One-Monitor Setup That Doesn't Feel Like a Compromise
summary: Not everyone needs three screens. Here's how to build a single-monitor setup that still feels deliberate rather than minimal-by-necessity.
content: A single well-chosen monitor at the right height and distance often outperforms a sprawling multi-screen setup for focus-heavy work — the constraint forces better window management instead of spreading attention across panels.

gear_list.name: Logitech Lift Vertical Ergonomic Wireless Mouse
